modules.title: EVM aur Solidity ki Gully - Base Sambhal Bhai
modules.description: Smart contracts ka internal mechanism samajhna, warna udne ke liye plane nahi rasta nahi milega.
objectives: EVM (Ethereum Virtual Machine) ka architecture aur opcode breakdown master karna
Solidity ke advanced concepts jaise memory, storage, aur assembly par pakki grip lena
Gas optimization aur EVM-level execution traps ko decode karna

modules.title: DeFi ke Kartoos - Dushman Ko Pehchano Guru
modules.description: Pehle hacks ka history samajh, tabhi pata chalega AI ko kya dhoondhna hai.
objectives: Reentrancy, Flash loans, aur Oracle manipulation jaise top 10 DeFi hacks dissect karna
SWC Registry ke vulnerabilities ko line-by-line analyze karna
Real-world bighacks (Ronin, Curve, Poly Network) ke post-mortem reports padhna

modules.title: AST aur Static Analysis - Blueprint Todh Ke Dekho
modules.description: Code ko sirf text mat samajh, uska tree structure (AST) todh ke loopholes nikaal.
objectives: Abstract Syntax Tree (AST) generate aur traverse karna Slither ya custom script se
Control Flow Graphs (CFG) banakar code execution paths map karna
Pattern matching rules likhna basic vulnerabilities catch karne ke liye

objectives: CodeBERT ya Solidity-specific transformers ke embeddings samajhna
Open-source LLMs (Llama 3, Mistral) ko Solidity code par test karna
Tokenization aur context window limits ko handle karna smart contracts ke liye

modules.title: Dataset ka Khazana - AI ko Asli Data Pilao
modules.description: Bina solid data ke AI bakwas karega, vulnerable aur safe contracts ka mega dataset jodo.
objectives: Etherscan aur DefiLlama se verified aur hacked contracts ka dataset scrape karna
Vulnerabilities ko label karna (Buggy/Not Buggy, CWE tags) manually ya heuristically
Data cleaning aur augmentation techniques lagana AI hallucination rokne ke liye

modules.title: LoRA aur Fine-Tuning - Asli Ghode Ko Sawar Karna
modules.description: Generic AI se kaam nahi chalega, Solidity auditor banane ke liye model ko custom train karo.
objectives: PEFT aur LoRA use karke LLM ko local ya cloud GPU par fine-tune karna
Instruction dataset banakar model ko 'Audit this code' prompt par train karna
Overfitting se bachna aur validation loss monitor karna

modules.title: Fuzzing aur Dynamic Analysis - Paka Ke Test Karo
modules.description: Sirf padhne se nahi, code ko chala ke panga lena padta hai scene clear karne ke liye.
objectives: Echidna ya Foundry Invariants use karke smart contracts ko fuzz karna
AI-generated inputs use karke edge cases trigger karna
Static (AI) aur Dynamic (Fuzzer) analysis ka fusion pipeline banana

modules.description: AI ko latest hack ka knowledge do, internet ka access do aur autonomous banao.
objectives: Vector database (Pinecone/Chroma) me SWC registry aur hack reports daalna
LangChain ya LlamaIndex pe RAG pipeline set up karna live context ke liye
AI Agent banana jo code dekhe, vulnerability search kare, aur fix suggest kare

modules.title: False Positives ka Kachra - Cleanup Boss
modules.description: AI galat alert dega toh devs gaali denge. Precision badhao ya ghar baitho.
objectives: Confidence threshold set karna aur low-confidence alerts filter karna
Heuristic checks lagana AI output ko cross-verify karne ke liye
Human-in-the-loop (HITL) feedback mechanism banana model ko continuously improve karne ke liye

modules.title: CI/CD aur Deployment - Asli Aukaat Dikhao
modules.description: Tool ko GitHub me ghusa do, har PR pe automatic audit ho, asli production scene.
objectives: GitHub Actions ya GitLab CI me AI auditor ko integrate karna
CLI tool banana jo devs easily apne local ya pipeline me chala sakein
Dashboard banana audit reports aur gas optimization tips visualize karne ke liye

modules.title: EVM aur Solidity ki Gully - Base Sambhal Bhai
modules.content:
Abe sun, raat ke 3 baj rahe hain. Tu coffee peeta hai, Slack pe "Deploying to mainnet" likh raha hai. Tera dost bolta hai "Bro, gas limit badha de, ho jayega." Tu badha diya. Transaction pending. Phir revert. 5 ETH gas fee ud gaya. BC, ek minute mein tera saal bhar ka internship stipend ud gaya! Rona a raha hai? Rona mat, gyaan sun.

Saale, tu AI auditor banna chahta hai. Solidity ke code mein se hacks nikalne ka sapna dekhta hai. Par sun, ek minute ruk. Tu jis building ka scanner banega, agar uski foundation (EVM) tujhe pata hi nahi, toh tu scanner nahi, scammer banega. 

Dekh bhai, ye aise samajh. Tu Mumbai ki local train mein first time baith raha hai. Koi tujhe Virar fast aur Churchgate slow ka difference samjhaye bina, tujhe Borivali pe utaar dega. EVM wahi local train hai. Agar iska route map (opcodes) aur engine (stack) tujhe pata nahi, toh tu blockchain pe kahin ka nahi rahega. 

Aaj Module 1 shuru kar rahe hain. Ye foundation hai. Isko skip kiya toh tere AI model ka dimaag dahi ban jayega. Baith, chai pi, aur dhyaan se padh.

## Asli Funda (Core Concepts)

### 1. EVM (Ethereum Virtual Machine) - Ek Global Khopcha

Bhai, EVM koi jadoo nahi hai. Ye ek sasta sa, dumb sa computer hai jo poore duniya mein chal raha hai. Har node (computer) pe ye EVM chalta hai. Tu Solidity mein code likhta hai, compiler usko machine language mein badalta hai, aur EVM usko chalata hai.

Asli scene kya hai? EVM ek "State Machine" hai. Iska matlab? 
> 💡 **Pro Tip:** EVM sirf state (halat) ko change karta hai. Pehle account A ke paas 10 ETH the (State 1). A ne B ko 2 ETH bheje. Ab A ke paas 8 ETH aur B ke paas 2 ETH (State 2). EVM bas ye calculation karke poore network ko bolta hai, "Bhai, naya state update kar lo."

EVM ke andar ka structure dekh, bilkul tapri wali setting hai:
1.  **Stack:** EVM ka dimaag. 1024 items ka limit. Yahan saari calculations hoti hain. LIFO (Last In First Out). 
2.  **Memory:** Temporary kaam ke liye. Function call ke liye data yahan aata hai. Function khatam, memory bhi khatam.
3.  **Storage:** Permanent database. Yeh blockchain pe likh chalta hai. Mahanga hai saala, ek byte likhne ka 20,000 gas lagta hai. Yahan tera pura contract ka data (state variables) rehta hai.
4.  **Calldata:** Jab koi bahar se transaction bhejta hai, toh input data yahan aata hai. Read-only hai. Sasta hai.

Samjha? Nahi samajh? Padh dubara, dimag lagaa. Ye charo cheezein agar tujhe clear nahi hain, toh AI ko kya sikhaayega tu? AI ko bolke kya padhayega? "Bhai storage aur memory same hai?" BC, same nahi hai! Storage blockchain pe hai, memory RAM pe. 

### 2. Opcodes - EVM ki Machine Language

Solidity likhta tu hai, par samajhta EVM kya hai? Opcodes. Ye 1-byte instructions hain. Jaise tere TV ka remote, har button ka apna code hota hai, EVM ke bhi 140+ opcodes hain.

Kuch main ke opcodes dekh, inko dimaag mein chapa (tattoo bana le):
*   `PUSH1 0x80`: Stack mein 0x80 daal de.
*   `MSTORE`: Memory mein kuch store kar.
*   `SSTORE`: Storage mein likh de. (Ye sabse mahanga, yahi pe hacker tera paisa bachata hai ya udata hai).
*   `CALL`: Dusre contract ko bulane ke liye. (DeFi hacks ka 80% yahin se aate hain, baad mein dekhenge).
*   `DELEGATECALL`: Sabse khatarnak. "Mera storage, tera logic." Ye external contract ko tera context deke bulata hai.

> ⚠️ **Common Mistake:** Log sochte hain Solidity seekhne se kaam ho jayega. Saale, Solidity sirf mask hai. Asli khel opcodes aur EVM bytecode ka hai. Jab tu AI ko train karega, usko bytecode aur opcode level ka data dena padega, warna wo surface-level bakwas karega.

### 3. Gas Mechanics - Har Saans Ka Bill

EVM pe kuch bhi free nahi hai. Saanp bhi cheerne ka paisa lagta hai. Har opcode ka apna gas cost hai. 
*   `ADD` (Jodna): 3 gas.
*   `SSTORE` (Storage mein likhna): 20,000 gas.
*   `SLOAD` (Storage padhna): 2,100 gas (cold access).

Agar tera smart contract gas limit cross kar gaya, toh EVM bolta hai "Out of Gas". Transaction revert ho jata hai. Lekin... aur yeh bada lekin hai... gas jo burn ho gaya, wo **refund nahi hoga**. Miner le jayega tera paisa.

> 🎯 **Key Insight:** Hacker ko gas ki parwah nahi hoti. Wo deliberately gas limit ko full karke DoS (Denial of Service) attack karte hain. Ek famous hack tha, tere jaise hi kisi ne loop mein storage update kiya, hacker ne us loop ko infinite bana diya. Contract gas out. Poora DeFi protocol freeze. Paisa lock hamesha ke liye. Isliye gas optimization sirf paise bachane ke liye nahi, security ke liye bhi zaroori hai.

### 4. Solidity ke Advanced Jhaat - Memory vs Storage

Bhai, 80% bugs yahin pe aate hain. C++ ya Java mein tu pointer bhul jaata hai, segmentation fault aata hai, life chali jaati hai. Solidity mein tu storage aur memory mix kar deta hai, toh poora blockchain ud jaata hai.

Dekh is code ko. Ye classic reentrancy ka mini version hai:

```solidity
// SPDX-License-Identifier: MIT
pragma solidity ^0.8.0;

contract Bank {
    mapping(address => uint) public balances;

    function deposit() public payable {
        balances[msg.sender] += msg.value;
    }

    function withdraw() public {
        uint bal = balances[msg.sender]; // Storage se Memory mein laaya
        require(bal > 0, "Khaali hai bhai");
        
        (bool sent, ) = msg.sender.call{value: bal}(""); // Paise bheje
        require(sent, "Fail ho gaya");
        
        balances[msg.sender] = 0; // Storage update kiya
    }
}
```

Abe dekh. Line by line. 
`balances[msg.sender]` ek storage variable hai (blockchain pe). 
Humne `uint bal = balances[msg.sender]` likha. Ab `bal` memory mein hai. 
Phir humne `msg.sender.call` se paise bheje. 
Aur last mein storage update kiya `balances[msg.sender] = 0`.

Saale, ye galat sequence hai! Agar `msg.sender` ek smart contract hai, toh `call` ke time pe wo wapas tere `withdraw` function ko call kar sakta hai (kyunki paise receive karne ke liye uske paas `receive()` ya `fallback()` function hoga). Aur kyunki tera storage abhi update nahi hua hai (`balances[msg.sender] = 0` abhi execute nahi hua), wo dobara `bal` dekhega, wahi purana balance milega, aur dobara paise nikal lega. Loop chalega jab tak contract poori tarah khali na ho jaye. 

> ☕ **Real Talk:** Ye Reentrancy attack hai. 2016 mein DAO hack yahi hua tha, 60 million dollars ud gaye the. Isliye **Checks-Effects-Interactions** pattern yaad rakh. Pehle check kar (require), phir state change kar (storage update), phir bahar interaction kar (paise bhej). Upar wale code mein paise bhejne se pehle `balances[msg.sender] = 0` likh de, hack ho jayega khatam.

### 5. Assembly (Yul) - EVM ke Kacche Kele

Jab Solidity ka code EVM ko samajh nahi aata, ya tu khud EVM ko direct control karna chahta hai, tab `assembly` use karte hain. Isko Yul bolte hain aaj kal.

Solidity compiler khud bhi aalsi hai. Kuch jagah pe wo extra gas kharch karta hai. Wahan pe gas bachane ke liye Assembly likhte hain. Par dhyan se, Assembly mein safety belt nahi hai. Tu khud stack manage karta hai.

```solidity
assembly {
    let result := add(2, 3) // Stack pe 2 aur 3 daal, add kar
    mstore(0x80, result)   // Memory ke 0x80 location pe store kar
}
```

Bhai, AI auditor banne ke liye tujhe Assembly pe nazar rakhni padegi. Kyunki hacker hamesha simple Solidity se nahi aata. Wo Assembly ke through overflow ya underflow karta hai. `0.8.0` se pehle Solidity mein integer overflow ka bug tha. 
Agar `uint8` max limit 255 hai. Agar tu 255 mein 1 jodega, toh zero ban jayega. Assembly level pe aaj bhi ye hota hai agar tu `unchecked` block use kare. AI ko ye pattern pakadne aana chahiye.

## Practical Scene (Street-level Application)

Ab theory band. Asli gully mein kaise kaam aayega ye sab? Tere AI auditor ka dimaag kaise sochega? 

Maan le tu ek AI model bana raha hai. Usko ek smart contract de rahe ho audit karne ke liye. AI ko kya steps follow karne hain? 

**Step 1: Bytecode Disassembly**
Saale, blockchain pe source code nahi milta hamesha. Bahut saare contracts verified nahi hote. Tujhe bytecode se source code ka andaza lagana padega. Tere AI ko opcode sequence pehle samajhna chahiye. 
Agar AI ko bytecode mein `0xf1` (CALL) aur `0xf4` (DELEGATECALL) dikhe, toh usko lal baati jal jaani chahiye. "Bhai, ye contract bahar kisi aur ko bula raha hai, yahan check kar."

**Step 2: Storage Layout Mapping**
AI ko pata hona chahiye ki contract ke state variables storage mein kahan store honge. 
Pehle slot 0, phir slot 1... par packing ka scene hai. 
Agar contract mein `uint128 a` aur `uint128 b` hai, toh dono ek hi storage slot (slot 0) mein pack ho jayenge. Kyunki 128 + 128 = 256 bits (1 slot = 256 bits).
Agar AI ye calculate nahi kar paya, toh wo galat jagah pe data read/write karega. Storage collision ho jayega. Proxy contracts mein ye sabse zyada hota hai.

**Step 3: Gas Trap Detection**
AI ko code dekh ke turant bolna chahiye, "Bhai, is loop mein tu storage read kar raha hai. Agar array ka size bada hua, toh gas limit cross ho jayegi."
```solidity
for (uint i = 0; i < users.length; i++) {
    users[i].balance += 1; // Yahan SLOAD aur SSTORE ho raha hai loop mein!
}
```
Ye code dekh ke AI ko gussa aana chahiye. "Saale, `users` ko memory mein le le, update kar, phir ek baar mein storage mein daal de. Har baar storage pe jaana mat!"

> 💡 **Pro Tip:** AI ko sikhate waqt, sirf Solidity code mat de. Uske saath EVM bytecode ka mapping de. Usko dikhao ki is Solidity line ka kaunsa opcode bana. Isse wo pattern recognition mein expert banega. "Oh, ye `delegatecall` wala opcode pattern toh DAO hack jaisa lagta hai, alert maaro!"

**Step 4: Execution Context (msg.sender vs tx.origin)**
Ek aur classic galti. 
`tx.origin` wo address hai jisne transaction shuru ki thi.
`msg.sender` wo address hai jisne direct tujhe call kiya.

Agar tu likhega:
```solidity
require(tx.origin == owner, "Not owner");
```
Toh tu gyaan baant raha hai hacker ko. Agar owner kisi malicious contract se interact karta hai, toh wo malicious contract tera ye function call karega. `msg.sender` malicious contract hoga, par `tx.origin` abhi bhi owner hoga! Authorization pass ho jayega. Phishing attack ho jayega. AI ko ye line dekh te hi chappal utaar ke maarna chahiye user ke dimaag pe. "BC, `msg.sender` use kar!"

Bhai, aaj ka chapter yahan khatam. Tere dimaag ka foundation EVM ke concrete pe set ho gaya hai. Agar tujhe lag raha hai ki ye bahot basic tha, toh tereko pata nahi kya nasha kar raha. Advanced AI auditor ke liye ye "basic" concepts hi toh hacking ke root cause hain. 

Agli module mein (DeFi ke Kartoos) hum history dekhenge. Jo hacks honge, unka post-mortem. Kyunki bina dushman ki history jaane, usse ladai nahi jeeti jaati. EVM ka jo dimaag aaj banaya, agle chapter mein usko hacks ke radar pe chalayenge. Tab tak ye chapter 3 baar padh. Code copy karke Remix pe chala. Varna agle chapter mein main chalaunga tujhe.

modules.title: DeFi ke Kartoos - Dushman Ko Pehchano Guru
modules.content:
Abe sun na, kal raat 3 baje ek banda Mumbai local mein chadhta hai. Virar fast. Bheed itni zyada ki agar tu aage se girega toh piche wale ko bhi sath leke girna padega. Ek chhokra aaram se khada hai, haath mein phone, Binance app khula hai. Achanak uska chehra utna udas ho gaya jaise exam mein paper milne ke baad hota hai. 10 lakh ke tokens... poof! Gayab. Usne screen pe dekha — "Transaction Executed". Uska smart contract hack ho gaya. Bhai ne apna aansu ponchha aur socha, "Aaj toh scam ho gaya, par kal main AI banaunga jo aise scams rokega."

Saale, wahi chhokra tu hai. Aur wahi Binance app wala scene tujhe bachana hai.

Pichle chapter mein humne EVM ki gully mein ghoomna seekha. Stack, Memory, Storage, aur Calldata ka poora khel samajh liya. Tujhe pata hai ki bytecode kaise banta hai aur gas trap kaise kaam karta hai. Par bhai, sirf engine samajhna kaafi nahi hai, tujhe dushman ki goli kahan se aayegi, ye bhi pata hona chahiye. Isliye Module 2 mein hum direct DeFi ke kartoos ka post-mortem karenge. Pehle dushman ko pehchan, tabhi tu usko AI se thok sakta hai. Nahi toh tera AI bhi wahi karega jo tu karta hai—chai peete hue bug ignore karna.

Ready? Collar pakad, chal.

## Asli Funda (Core Concepts)

Dekh bhai, DeFi ka funda simple hai. Code law hai. Par jab code mein galti hoti hai, toh koi judge nahi aata bail dene. Seedha account zero. Aur ye galtiyan randomly nahi hoti. Inka ek pattern hota hai. Agar tujhe AI banake hacker ka popat banana hai, toh tujhe in patterns ki DNA level pe pehchan honi chahiye. Hum top 3 dushmanon ko dissect karenge, aur baaki sabko SWC registry ke thoko se sikhenge.

### 1. Reentrancy: Andar Ghuske Baahar Nikalna (The Ultimate Jugaad)

Saale, Reentrancy itna purana hack hai ki isko dada ji bhi jaante hain. 2016 mein DAO hack hua tha, aur aaj tak log isko reproduce karte hain. Kya maa ch*d raha hai sacchi mein? Pehli baar mein hi 60 million dollars ud gaya tha.

**Chai ki tapri wali explanation:** Bhai, ye aise samajh. Tu tapri pe vada pav khata hai. Tu tapri wale ko bolta hai, "Bhaiya, paise deta hoon, pehle pav de do." Tu pav khaata hai. Fir tu bolta hai, "Bhaiya, paise de diye the, ek aur pav de do." Bhaiya tera balance check nahi karta, aur tu 10 baar pav khaa jaata hai. Balance minus nahi hua, aur tu unlimited vada pav khaa gaya. Hacker bhi exactly yahi karta hai.

Tujhe pata hai EVM mein `Checks-Effects-Interactions` pattern hona chahiye (jo humne Module 1 mein baat ki thi). Par devs chutiye hote hain, pehle interaction (pav dena) kar dete hain, baad mein effect (balance minus karna). Hacker iska fayda uthata hai.

Dekh is ghatiya code ko:

```solidity
// ⚠️ Common Mistake: Pehle paise de diye, baad mein balance update kiya. BC, seedha loot!
contract ChomuVendingMachine {
    mapping(address => uint) public balance;
    
    function withdraw() public {
        uint amount = balance[msg.sender]; // 1. Check
        (bool success, ) = msg.sender.call{value: amount}(""); // 2. Interaction (Pav de diya)
        require(success, "Transfer failed");
        balance[msg.sender] = 0; // 3. Effect (Ab balance zero kiya, par bohot late ho gaya bhai)
    }
}
```

Ab hacker ka contract dekh:

```solidity
// Hacker ka contract
contract Hacker {
    ChomuVendingMachine target;
    
    constructor(address _target) {
        target = ChomuVendingMachine(_target);
    }
    
    // Jab target paise bhejega, ye fallback trigger hoga
    receive() external payable {
        if (address(target).balance >= 1 ether) {
            target.withdraw(); // BC, fir se withdraw bol raha hai! Infinite loop!
        }
    }
    
    function attack() public {
        target.deposit{value: 1 ether}(); // Pehle 1 ether daala
        target.withdraw(); // Fir withdraw maanga... aur shuru ho gaya tamasha
    }
}
```

**Kaise hua?** `withdraw()` call hua. Paise `msg.sender.call` se hacker ke contract gaye. Hacker ke contract ka `receive()` function trigger hua. Usne andar hi `target.withdraw()` fir se call kar diya. Ab EVM wapas `ChomuVendingMachine` mein gaya, par `balance[msg.sender] = 0` line tak pahuncha hi nahi tha! Balance abhi bhi 1 ether tha. Toh usne firse paise bheje. Ye silsila tab tak chala jab tak contract khali nahi hua. Samjha? Nahi samjha? Padh dubara BC.

Tera AI ko yeh logic pakadna hai. Jab `call` ke baad state update ho raha ho, aur us `call` mein `msg.sender` ya external address trigger kar raha ho, toh wo reentrancy ka gunda hai.

> ⚠️ **Common Mistake:** Devs sochte hain ki "Arre bhai, maine `msg.sender.call` use kiya hai, gas limit handle kar lega." Saale, gas limit aur reentrancy mein zameen aasman ka farq hai. `call` gives up control. Agar state update baad mein hai, tu gaya. Tera AI ko ye gas trap detection se nahi, flow analysis se pakadna hai.

### 2. Flash Loans: Ek Raat Ka Raja

BC, flash loan sunne mein aisa lagta hai jaise IPL mein ek match mein hi team ko trophy dilwa do. Bhai, DeFi ka sabse khatarnak weapon hai ye. Normal loan mein bank manager tera CIBIL score check karta hai. Flash loan mein koi nahi poochta. Tu 100 crore dollar bhi le sakta hai, bina collateral ke, bas ek condition — saara paisa usi transaction mein wapas aa jana chahiye. Agar wapas nahi aaya, toh poora transaction revert ho jayega. Jaise hi tu transaction start kiya, tere paas 100 crore hain. Transaction khatam hone pe 100 crore wapas. Bech mein tu jo karega, wo tera marzi.

**Desi Analogy:** Ye aise hai jaise tu shadi mein baraat leke gaya. Dekha ki dulhan side mein hai. Dulhan ke ghar wale 1 din ke liye tujhe sheher ka sabse bada jeweller bana dete hain. Tu us sehra pehen ke jaata hai, us 1 din ke andar poore sheher ka sona kharid leta hai, market manipulate karta hai, aur raat ko shehra utaar ke wapas kar deta hai. Agar tu shehra wapas nahi karta, toh baarat wapas nahi jaayegi (revert). Par jeweller ne paisa le liya, market ki gaand maar li.

Flash loan attack ka target hota hai Price Oracles. Oracle basically ek bhai hai jo bahar ki duniya ki price contract ko batata hai (jaise ETH ki price $2000 hai). Par dev sochta hai, "Arre bhai, main Uniswap pool ki price use karunga oracle ke liye." Saale, Uniswap pool ka price flash loan se hila toh sakte hain na?

**Kaise hota hai attack?**
1. Hacker flash loan se 100M USD borrow karta hai.
2. Uniswap pe jaaakar poora sasta token kharid leta hai. Isse token ki price artificially 10x ho gayi.
3. Ab ye artificially inflated price tera chutiya smart contract oracle read karta hai. Contract sochta hai, "Waah, token itna mehenga hai!" Aur us price ke hisaab se hacker ko loan de deta hai (ya koi collateral liquidate kar deta hai).
4. Hacker wapas token Uniswap pe bech deta hai, price normal ho jati hai.
5. Flash loan wapas. Profit pocket.

Bhai, bade bade protocols isme fas gaye. bZx, Harvest Finance, Cheese Bank. Sab ne oracle ko Uniswap pe chhod diya tha.

> 💡 **Pro Tip:** Tere AI ko train karte waqt, `getPrice()` ya `latestAnswer()` jaise functions ka analysis karna. Agar wo price direct DEX (Decentralized Exchange) se laa raha hai bina Time-Weighted Average Price (TWAP) ke, toh wo flash loan ka shikaar ban sakta hai. TWAP ka funda samajh — price ko multiple blocks pe average karo, taaki ek block ka manipulation damage na kar sake.

### 3. Oracle Manipulation: Aankh Band Karke Vishwas

Oracle manipulation flash loan ka bada bhai hai. Dekh, smart contract andar se bahar ki duniya nahi dekh sakta. Usko kisi ko bhejna padta hai data laane. Ye data laane wala bhai hai Oracle.

Agar tera contract ek hi Oracle pe depend karta hai, aur us Oracle ne galti ki ya usko hack kar liya, toh tera contract bhi mar jayega. Ya agar Oracle DEX se price laa raha hai, toh flash loan se manipulate ho jayega.

Chainlink jake centralized oracle use karne chahiye, ya TWAP use karna chahiye. Par devs kabhi kabhi `msg.sender` ya single source pe bharosa kar lete hain. Tera AI ko ye pattern pakadna hai — single point of failure.

### 4. SWC Registry: Hacker ki Playbook

Saale, tujhe dushman ki bhasha samajhni padegi. SWC (Smart Contract Weakness Classification) registry hacker ki playbook hai. Ye basically OWASP hai Solidity ka. Isme har vulnerability ka ek ID hai. Agar tujhe AI ka model train karna hai, toh SWC IDs tere labels honge.

Chal, kuch khaas kartoos dekh:

**SWC-101: Integer Overflow and Underflow**
Pehle ke zamaane mein (Solidity <0.8.0), agar tu `uint8` mein 255 store karta hai aur 1 add karta, toh wo 0 ho jata. Jaise autorickshaw ka meter 99,999 se 00,000 pe aaye. BC, iska fayda uthake log token balance zero karwa lete the. Ab Solidity 0.8.0+ mein default check aa gaya hai. Par purane contracts, ya `unchecked` block use karne wale, abhi bhi mare hue hain.

```solidity
// SWC-101 example
function transfer(address _to, uint _value) public {
    // Agar _value balance se zyada hai, toh purane version mein underflow hoke
    // attacker ke paas max uint tokens aa jayenge
    balances[msg.sender] -= _value; 
    balances[_to] += _value;
}
```

Tera AI ko `unchecked` keyword dekhate hi alarm bajna chahiye. "Boss, yahan goli chal sakti hai, dhyan se dekh."

**SWC-107: Reentrancy**
Jo upar detailed kiya, wahi SWC-107 hai. Tera AI ko `call{value:...}` ya `transfer()` ke aas-paas state changes dhoondhne hain. Checks-Effects-Interactions rule todne wale functions ko flag karna hai.

**SWC-116: Block Values as Proxy for Time**
Devs kabhi kabhi `block.timestamp` use karte hain random number ya time-lock ke liye. Saale, miner `block.timestamp` ko thoda manipulate kar sakta hai (15 seconds ke andar). Agar tu lottery contract bana raha hai `block.timestamp % 2 == 0` pe, toh miner aur hacker milkar tujhje chutiya bana sakte hain.

> 🎯 **Key Insight:** SWC registry tere AI ke liye ground truth hai. Jab tu model train karega, har vulnerable code snippet ko SWC ID tag karna. "Ye code SWC-107 hai." Isse tera AI sikh jayega ki "Bhai, ye pattern dekha hai, ye 107 hai, alert maaro." Bina SWC ke tu AI ko random gaaliyan dila raha hoga, kuch nahi seekhega.

### 5. Access Control: Darwaza Khula Chhodna

Bhai, soch. Tu ghar mein sota hai aur darwaza khula chhodta hai? Nahi na? Par devs smart contract mein aksar `public` ya `external` chhod dete hain functions ko. 

Sabse famous example hai Parity Multisig Wallet hack. Ek chhota sa function `initWallet` public tha. Kisi bhi bande ne usko call kiya, khud ko owner bana liya, aur poore contract ka control le liya. Fir usne `kill` function call kiya, aur 150 million dollars lock ho gaye forever. Darwaza khula chhodne ki sazaa mil gayi.

Tera AI ko function visibility check karni hai. Agar koi `onlyOwner` modifier nahi hai aur function sensitive state change kar raha hai, toh wo potential gunda hai.

## Practical Scene (Street-level Application)

Ab tu soch raha hoga, "Bhai, theory toh mast hai. Par ye sab AI ko kaise pakrayenge?" Dekh, tera AI ek brain hai. Usko experience chahiye. Bina solid data ke AI bakwas karega, vulnerable aur safe contracts ka mega dataset jodo — ye Module 5 ka kaam hai. Par abhi hum dekhte hain ki real-world hacks ki post-mortem report padh ke humein kya seekhna hai. Kyunki bhai, bina dushman ko jaane, tu usko kabhi nahi harayega.

### Post-Mortem 1: Ronin Bridge Hack ($625M - Sabse Bada Heist)

Bhai, 2022 ka sabse bada loot. Axie Infinity ka Ronin Bridge. 625 million dollars ud gaye. Ek second soch, itne paise mein tu poore Mumbai ka real estate kharid sakta hai. Par hack kaise hua? Solidity mein bug tha? Nahi saale! Social engineering thi.

Ronin Bridge 9 validator nodes pe depend karta tha. Hack karega toh 5 ko compromise karna padega. Hacker ne ek fake job offer LinkedIn pe daala. Ek dev ne PDF download kiya (job offer ka). Us PDF mein trojan tha. Uske through hacker ne 4 validator keys chura liye. Ek validator Axie DAO ka tha, jiska API unhone approve kar rakha tha free use ke liye. Hacker ne us API ka use karke 5th signature le liya. Boom. Withdraw approved.

> ☕ **Real Talk:** Tera AI sirf Solidity code nahi padhega. Tujhe ye samajhna padega ki hacks sirf code level pe nahi hote. Infrastructure level pe bhi hote hain. Par tere AI ka focus code level pe hoga, isliye tujhe pata hona chahiye ki "Bridge" architecture kaise kaam karta hai. Multi-sig wallets ka logic kaise hota hai. Tera AI ko `require(signatures.length >= THRESHOLD)` jaise checks dhoondhne hain, aur check karna hai ki threshold kitna safe hai. Agar threshold 1 hai 10 mein se, toh bhai, ghar ka darwaza khula hai.

### Post-Mortem 2: Curve Finance Hack ($70M - Compiler ki Galti)

Ye hack sabse zyada interesting hai. Curve ka code mathematically sound tha. Auditor ne bhi check kiya tha. Par Vyper compiler mein bug tha! Vyper ek language hai Solidity jaisa, par iska compiler version 0.2.15 mein ek reentrancy lock ka issue tha.

Curve ne reentrancy lock use kiya tha. Par compiler ne us lock ka bytecode galat generate kiya. Lock kaam nahi karega, aur hacker ko reentrancy ka chance mil gaya. 70 million dollars gayab.

**Tera AI kya seekhega isse?** Tera AI sirf `.sol` file nahi padhega. Usko compiler version bhi check karna hoga. Agar koi `pragma solidity ^0.4.0` use kar raha hai, toh tera AI ko pata hona chahiye ki is version mein `delegatecall` ka storage collision bug hai. Compiler versions ki history tere AI ke dimag mein honi chahiye. Jaise naye phone mein purana app chalane ki warning aati hai, waise tera AI bolega — "Bhai, ye compiler version purana hai, isme yeh bugs confirmed hain, upgrade kar."

### Post-Mortem 3: Poly Network Hack ($610M - Cross-Chain Confusion)

Saale, 610 million dollars ka hack! Isme hacker ne ek baar mein itna paisa nikala ki crypto ki history split ho gayi. Poly Network ek cross-chain bridge protocol tha. Iska funda tha alag-alag chains ke beech mein token transfer karna.

Hack kaise hua? Cross-chain management contract mein ek function tha jo custom logic call karta tha. Par usme access control ki galti thi. Ek function `_verifyHeaderAndExecuteTx` tha, jo kisi specific cross-chain message ko verify karke execute karta tha. Par usme ek parameter tha `keepEth` jise hacker ne bypass kar diya. Hacker ne ek fake message construct kiya, jisme usne khud ko owner bana liya, aur contract ke paas jitne bhi tokens the, wo sab apne wallet mein transfer kar liye.

**Tera AI kya seekhega?** Cross-chain bridges sabse zyada vulnerable hote hain. Kyunki unka logic complex hota hai. Ek side pe verify, doosri side pe execute. Tera AI ko `delegatecall` aur `selfdestruct` jaise opcodes pe extra nazar rakhni hai. Agar koi function user input ke basis pe `delegatecall` ka target address decide karta hai, toh wo 100% gunda hai.

> ⚠️ **Common Mistake:** Aise mat soch ki "Arre bhai, mera contract toh simple ERC20 hai, isme hack kya hoga?" Saale, agar tere ERC20 token ko koi DEX list karta hai, aur tere token mein ek hidden mint function hai, toh hacker DEX ka pool drain kar dega. Tera token safe hai, par DEX ka liquidity gayab. Context samajh. AI ko context dena padega.

### Post-Mortem 4: Wormhole Bridge ($326M - Signature Verification Bypass)

Wormhole Solana-Ethereum bridge tha. Is hack mein hacker ne Solana side pe fake signature generate kiya. Wormhole ka contract ye assume karta tha ki jo signature Solana se aaya hai, wo valid hai. Par unhone signature verification ke pehle ek crucial step miss kiya. Hacker ne "Guardian" set fake karke inject kar diya. Uske baad toh khullam khulla mint karke Ethereum pe transfer kar diya.

**Tera AI kya seekhega?** Signature verification sabse zyada tricky hai. `ecrecover` function Solidity mein zero address return karta hai agar signature invalid hai. Devs kabhi `require(ecrecover(...) != address(0))` check karna bhool jate hain. Tera AI ko har `ecrecover` call pe nazar rakhni hai. Agar uske baad proper validation nahi hai, toh flag karna.

### The AI Auditor's Mindset: Kya Dhoondhna Hai?

Ab tu soch raha hoga, "Bhai, itne hacks, itne patterns. Mera AI kaise sab yaad rakhega?" Saale, isliye toh hum AI bana rahe hain na! Par AI ko sikhane se pehle, tujhe pattern samajhna hai. Tera AI ko char cheezon pe focus karna hoga:

1. **State Changes after External Calls:** Reentrancy ka root cause. Tera AI ko control flow graph banana aana chahiye. Jaise Module 1 mein humne storage layout mapping kiya, waise control flow map karna hai. Agar `call` ke baad state change, toh red flag.

2. **Oracle Dependencies:** Agar contract bahar se price laa raha hai, toh wo source kya hai? Single DEX? Centralized API? Tera AI ko data sources identify karne hain.

3. **Access Control & Visibility:** Sensitive functions pe modifier hai ya nahi? `public` functions jo state change karte hain, unko flag karna hai.

4. **Arithmetic Operations:** `unchecked` blocks, purane compiler versions, division before multiplication (precision loss). Ye sab mathematical gunde hain.

> 💡 **Pro Tip:** Jab tu AI ko code feed karega, toh sirf vulnerable code mat de. Safe code bhi de. Agar tu sirf vulnerable code dega, toh tera AI har cheez ko hack samajhega. Jaise wo chhokre jo heartbreak ke baad har ladki ko villain samajhte hain. Balance chahiye. Positive aur negative samples, dono chahiye.

### The SWC-116 Deep Dive: Time-based Vulnerabilities

Ek aur important scene samajh. `block.timestamp` aur `block.number` ka use. Devs kabhi kabhi time-based logic banate hain. Jaise "24 hours baad withdraw open hoga." Par bhai, `block.timestamp` miner ke haath mein hai. Miner usko 15 seconds aage-peeche kar sakta hai.

Agar tu staking contract bana raha hai, aur reward `block.timestamp` pe based hai, toh miner reward manipulate kar sakta hai. Tera AI ko `block.timestamp` arithmetic dikhna chahiye. Agar `block.timestamp` modulo ya division mein use ho raha hai, toh wo suspicious hai.

```solidity
// SWC-116 vulnerable code
function lottery() public {
    // BC, miner isko manipulate kar sakta hai!
    uint winningNumber = uint(block.timestamp) % 100;
    if (winningNumber == 69) {
        msg.sender.transfer(address(this).balance);
    }
}
```

Tera AI ko bolega: "Bhai, `block.timestamp` use ho raha hai randomness ke liye. SWC-116. Miners isko manipulate kar sakte hain. `blockhash` ya Chainlink VRF use karo."

### The Human Element: Social Engineering in Code

Bhai, ek baat samajh. Hacker sirf code nahi todta. Wo developer ki psychology samajhta hai. Devs lazy hote hain. Copy-paste karte hain Stack Overflow se. OpenZeppelin ka import karte hain bina samjhe. Tera AI ko in patterns pe bhi nazar rakhni hai.

Jaise, agar koi contract mein `Ownable` import hai, par `onlyOwner` modifier use nahi ho raha critical functions pe, toh dev bhul gaya. Tera AI ko aise inconsistencies dhoondhne hain. "Bhai, library import ki, par use nahi ki. Kya maa ch*d raha hai?"

### Real-World AI Auditor Workflow

Ab practical scene samajh. Tera AI kaise kaam karega?

1. **Code Ingestion:** User ne `.sol` file upload ki. Tera AI pehle AST (Abstract Syntax Tree) banaega. Ye Module 3 ka scene hai, par abhi itna samajh ki code ko tree form mein todhna hai.

2. **Pattern Matching:** AST mein SWC patterns dhoondhna. Jaise `call` ke baad state change = SWC-107.

3. **Data Flow Analysis:** Variable kahan se aaya, kahan tak gaya. Agar user input direct `delegatecall` mein ja raha hai, toh SWC-112 (Delegatecall to Untrusted Callee).

4. **Context Analysis:** Contract ka purpose samajhna. DEX hai? Lending protocol hai? Bridge hai? Har protocol ke apne risks hain.

5. **Report Generation:** AI bolega, "Bhai, line 42 pe reentrancy ka risk hai. Line 87 pe oracle manipulation. Line 112 pe access control missing. Total 3 critical, 2 medium. Fix kar, warna ud jayega."

> ☕ **Real Talk:** Tera AI pehle din se perfect nahi hoga. False positives aayenge. Saale, mere ko bhi aate hain. Par pattern recognition improve hoga. Module 9 mein hum false positives ka kachra saaf karenge. Abhi itna samajh ki AI ko dushman ke patterns (SWC registry) aur real-world hacks dono sikhane hain.

### The Ultimate Checklist for Your AI

Tera AI dimag mein ye checklist hona chahiye jab wo code dekhe:

- [ ] External calls ke baad state change? (Reentrancy)
- [ ] Price oracle single source pe dependent? (Oracle Manipulation)
- [ ] Flash loan se pool price manipulate ho sakta? (DEX Integration)
- [ ] Sensitive functions pe access control hai? (SWC-105)
- [ ] `block.timestamp` randomness ke liye use ho raha? (SWC-116)
- [ ] Arithmetic `unchecked` block mein? (SWC-101)
- [ ] Compiler version purana? (Known compiler bugs)
- [ ] `delegatecall` user-controlled address pe? (SWC-112)
- [ ] Signature verification proper hai? (SWC-122)
- [ ] Loop mein gas limit exceed ho sakta? (SWC-128)

Agar tera AI ye 10 cheezein check kar lega, toh wo 80% hacks rok lega. Baki 20% advanced logic errors hain, jo Module 7 mein fuzzing se pakde jayenge.

### Closing: Gully Ka Naseehat

Saale, sun lena. Ye chapter sirf history nahi hai. Ye tere AI ki training data hai. Agar tune in hacks ko achhe se nahi samjha, toh tera AI bhi wahi karega jo tu karta hai—chupchap bug ignore karna. Reentrancy, Flash Loans, Oracle Manipulation, Access Control. Ye charo tere AI ke dimag mein cement ho jana chahiye.

SWC registry ko apni pocket Bible samajh. Har ID ek goli hai. Tera AI ko har goli pehchan aani chahiye. Real-world hacks ke post-mortem se seekh ki hacker kaise sochta hai. Hacker hamesha edge cases dhoondhta hai. Tera AI ko bhi edge cases pe focus karna hai.

Agli module mein hum AST ka tree structure todenge. Code ko line-by-line nahi, node-by-node analyze karenge. Kyunki bhai, code ko sirf text mat samajh, uska blueprint todh ke dekh. Tabhi loopholes nikaal paayega. Ab ja, ek chai pi, aur is chapter ko dubara padh. Kyunki agla module aur intense hoga, aur wahan tu akela nahi hoga—tera AI bhi tere sath hoga. Par tab tak, dushman ko pehchan. Samjha? Nahi samjha? Padh dubara BC.

modules.title: AST aur Static Analysis - Blueprint Todh Ke Dekho
modules.content:
Abe sun, raat ke 3 baje jab tu code likhte-likhte ro raha hai aur compiler error lafa deti hai "Unexpected Token" pe, tab lagta hai ki Solidity compiler teri ex jaisi hai—baat hi nahi samajhti. Tu line mein semicolon laga raha hai, aur compiler bhaiya bol rahe hain "Bhai kya bakwaas likh raha hai?" 

Saale, problem ye nahi ki tu dumb hai (well, thoda hai), problem ye hai ki tu code ko ekdum plain text ki tarah padh raha hai. Jaise tu "Sholay" ki script padh raha hai. "Yeh haath mujhe de de Thakur" — text mein simple lagta hai, lekin emotion, context, aur structure alag hai! 

Chapter 1 aur 2 mein tune EVM ka internal khel samjha, DeFi ke dushmano ke history (SWC-101, SWC-107) ko pehchaana. Ab agar tera AI saala Jai-Veeru banke hack rokna hai, toh usko code padhne ka asli tareeka aana chahiye. Plain text se nahi hoga, boss. 

Aaj hum code ki **Aukaat** dekhenge. Hum usko tod ke uska skeleton nikaalenge. Welcome to **AST aur Static Analysis**. Buckle up, kyunki ab dimaak ki makkhiyan udne wali hain.

---

## Asli Funda (Core Concepts)

### 1. AST (Abstract Syntax Tree) - Code ka X-Ray

Dekh bhai, tera AI model LLM hai. Wo language model hai, calculator nahi. Agar tu usko raw Solidity code dunga, toh wo usko English ki tarah padhega. "Oh bhai, `msg.sender` likha hai, must be sender ka address." BC, usko ye nahi pata ki ye `msg.sender` actually kahan call ho raha hai aur kiska balance change kar raha hai. 

Code ko machine ko samjhane ke liye, hum usko ek **Tree (Ped)** ki tarah todte hain. Isko bolte hain **AST (Abstract Syntax Tree)**.

> ☕ **Real Talk:** Agar tu AST nahi samjhega, toh tera AI kabhi bhi false positive dega. Wo bas pattern match karega `transfer` word dekh ke, aur bolegi "Reentrancy hai!" Saale, har `transfer` reentrancy nahi hota. Context chahiye, aur context AST se aata hai.

**Desi Analogy:** 
AST aise samajh, jaise tu autorickshaw ko alag-alag parts mein tod raha hai. 
- Poori gaadi = Source Code
- Body = Contract Definition
- Engine = Function Definition  
- Petrol tank = State Variables
- Self-starter = Function Call

Jab tu code ko AST mein convert karta hai, toh compiler usko nested nodes (json format) mein baant deta hai. 

Maan le ekdum basic code:
```solidity
contract ChaiTapri {
    uint public cupPrice = 10;

    function buyChai(uint cups) public {
        uint total = cups * cupPrice;
    }
}
```

Ab iska AST kaisa dikhega? Aise:
- `ContractDefinition` (Name: ChaiTapri)
  - `VariableDeclaration` (Name: cupPrice, Type: uint)
  - `FunctionDefinition` (Name: buyChai)
    - `Block` (Function body)
      - `VariableDeclarationStatement` (Name: total)
        - `BinaryOperation` (Operator: *)
          - `Identifier` (cups)
          - `Identifier` (cupPrice)

Samjha? Nahi? Padh dubara BC. 

> 🎯 **Key Insight:** AST mein har cheez ek "Node" hai. Function ek node hai, variable ek node hai, multiplication ek node hai. Tera AI ko bas in nodes ke relation ko padhna hai. "Kaunsa node, kis node ke andar hai?" Ye pata lagana hi Static Analysis ka asli game hai.

### 2. Slither aur Custom Scripts - Jugaad ka Baap

Ab manually AST banane ke liye tu compiler ke andar ghusne ka soch raha hai kya? Saale, gully ke don time waste nahi karte. Iske liye tools bane hain. Sabse khatarnak aur street-smart tool hai **Slither**.

Slither Solidity compiler (solc) ko chalaata hai aur uske AST ko ek Python object mein convert kar deta hai. Tu Python mein bas `contract.functions` likhega, aur tere saamne saare functions ki list aa jaayegi. Jaise tapri pe bas bolna "Ek chai", aur chai mil jaati hai. 

Slither se tere paas 3 cheezein aati hain:
1. **Contract objects:** Kaunse contract hai, inheritance kya hai.
2. **Function objects:** Function visibility kya hai, state variables kya use ho rahe hain.
3. **SlithIR:** Slither ka apna intermediate language. Ye AST se bhi ekdum deep jaata hai. Tera AI agar SlithIR samajh gaya, toh wo unstoppable hai.

Agar tu Slither use nahi karna chahta (kyunki tu rebel hai), toh `solc` ka `--ast-compact-json` flag use kar. Usse raw JSON mil jaayega. Phir tu Python ya TypeScript mein khud ka parser likh sakta hai. Lekin sach batau? Chai tapri pe khud patti peesne se acha hai Slither ka use karke ready-made chai pi lo. Focus tera AI model ko train karne pe hona chahiye, parser likhne pe nahi.

### 3. Control Flow Graphs (CFG) - Rasta Kahan Ja Raha Hai?

AST sirf structure batata hai ki "kya kya hai". Lekin ye nahi batata ki "kaise chalega". Code execution ka flow samajhne ke liye hum **CFG (Control Flow Graph)** banate hain.

**Desi Analogy:**
CFG aise samajh, jaise local train ka map. CST se karke Virar tak ki line hai. Har station ek node hai. Lekin Fast train kuch stations skip karti hai, aur Slow train sab pe rukti hai. `if/else` statements, `for` loops, aur `require()` aisi cheezein hain jo track change karti hain. 

Agar tere AI ko pata hona chahiye ki hack possible hai ya nahi, toh usko poora rasta (path) dekhna padega. 

Maan le function aisa hai:
```solidity
function withdraw(uint amount) public {
    if (amount > balances[msg.sender]) {
        revert("Kya maa ch*d raha hai?");
    }
    // ... further logic
}
```
Yahan CFG batayega ki agar `amount > balances` hai, toh flow `revert` pe jayega aur ruk jaayega. Agar nahi hai, toh aage ke `further logic` pe jayega. 

> ⚠️ **Common Mistake:** Bahut saale beginners AST dekh ke sochte hain "Arre, `revert` likha hai, toh safe hai!" BC, agar `if` condition galat hai, ya visibility `private` ki jagah `public` ho gayi, toh wo revert kabhi trigger nahi hoga. CFG banakar tujhe exact execution path pata chalega. Slither automatically CFG banata hai. Tera AI bas us graph ko traverse karna seekhega.

---

## Practical Scene (Street-level Application)

Ab theory band, asli gully mein utarte hain. Tera AI model banega kaise? Tujhe pattern matching rules likhni padengi. Chapter 2 mein humne SWC-107 (Reentrancy) aur SWC-101 (Integer Overflow) ke baare mein padha tha. Ab hum AST aur CFG use karke unhe catch karenge.

### Rule 1: Reentrancy Ka Shikar (Using Checks-Effects-Interactions)

Yaad kar, Chapter 2 mein humne **Checks-Effects-Interactions** rule baat ki thi. Agar interaction (external call) effects (state change) se pehle aaya, toh reentrancy hack possible hai.

Tera AI ko kaise pata chalega? Tera AI Slither se AST aur CFG nikalega, aur ek simple rule check karega:
1. Function ke andar `External Call` (jaise `.call{value: ...}("")` ya `.transfer()`) dhund.
2. Us external call ke *baad* (CFG ka next node), koi `State Variable` (jaise `balances[msg.sender] = 0`) update ho raha hai ya nahi?
3. Agar haan, toh ghusa do list mein: "Reentrancy Vulnerability Found!"

Code dekh:
```solidity
function withdraw() public {
    uint bal = balances[msg.sender];
    (bool success, ) = msg.sender.call{value: bal}(""); // INTERACTION
    
    if (success) {
        balances[msg.sender] = 0; // EFFECT (After interaction = BOOM!)
    }
}
```

Agar tu iska AST dekhega, toh `FunctionCall` node pehle aayega, aur `Assignment` node baad mein aayega. Slither ka `find_children` function use karke tera AI easily check kar lega ki state change external call ke baad ho raha hai ya nahi. 

> 💡 **Pro Tip:** Tere AI ko sirf "call" word nahi dhundhna hai. Usko ye check karna hai ki `msg.sender.call` hai ya koi random address. Kyunki agar fixed address pe call ho raha hai, toh reentrancy nahi ho sakti. Iske liye AST mein `Expression` node ka type check karna padega. Asli devil details mein hai, boss.

### Rule 2: Integer Overflow / Underflow (SWC-101) - Arithmetic Operations

Chapter 2 mein humne dekha ki agar `uint8` mein 255 store hai aur tu +1 karega, toh wo 0 ho jaayega. Bina `SafeMath` ya Solidity 0.8+ ke, ye guaranteed suicide hai.

Tera AI rule kaisa hoga?
1. AST mein saare `BinaryOperation` nodes dhund jisme operator `+`, `-`, ya `*` ho.
2. Check kar ki kya uske around `require()` ya `if` condition hai jo boundary check kar rahi ho?
3. Check kar ki Solidity version 0.8 se kam toh nahi? (Kyunki 0.8+ mein default overflow check hota hai).

Agar version <0.8 hai, aur aise hai:
```solidity
function multiply(uint a, uint b) public pure returns (uint) {
    return a * b; // SWC-101 red alert!
}
```
AST analysis batayega ki `BinaryOperation (*)` directly return ho raha hai, bina kisi `FunctionCall` (SafeMath) ya `IfStatement` (boundary check) ke. Tera AI turant flag kar dega.

### Rule 3: Access Control & Visibility - Bina Darwaza Lagaye Ghar

Saale, ek gully ka don kabhi bina guard ke apna godown nahi chhodta. Smart contract mein godam tere state variables hain, aur guard teri function visibility hai. 

Agar tere contract mein ek function hai jo owner ka balance update karta hai, aur uska visibility `public` hai, toh koi bhi aake apne ko owner bana lega. 

Tera AI kaise pakdega?
Slither se AST traverse karke bas check kar:
1. Function visibility kya hai? Agar `public` ya `external` hai.
2. Kya function ke andar `require(msg.sender == owner)` ya `onlyOwner` modifier use hua hai?
3. Agar nahi hua, aur function sensitive state changes (jaise `totalSupply` change) kar raha hai, toh ghol do sale ko.

> ⚠️ **Common Mistake:** Bahut log sochte hain "Arre bhai, function ka naam `internalWithdraw` rakha hai, toh public log isko call nahi karenge." BC, blockchain pe naam se kaam nahi chalta. Agar visibility public hai, toh hacker ke liye ye "externalWithdraw" hai. Tera AI ko naam se nahi, AST ke `Visibility` node se pata chalega ki ye public hai.

### Rule 4: Oracle Dependencies - Bhai ki Bolne Pe Mat Chal

Chapter 2 mein humne **Oracle Dependencies** baat ki thi. Agar tere contract ka price feed ek single centralized source (jaise Binance API) pe depend hai, toh wo hack ho sakta hai ya manipulat ho sakta hai. Flash loan attacks aise hi hote hain.

AST aur CFG ka use karke tera AI kya karega?
1. Function ke andar dekh ki koi `interface` ya `external call` se price fetch ho raha hai? (jaise `priceOracle.getPrice()`).
2. Agar price fetch ho raha hai, toh check kar ki kya us price ke against immediately swap, borrow, ya liquidity add karne ka function hai?
3. Agar haan, toh check kar ki kya koi circuit breaker hai? (jaise `require(price > 0 && price < maxLimit)`).

Agar direct price use ho raha hai bina check ke, toh AI bolega: "Bhai, ye oracle manipulate ho sakta hai. Flash loan se tera sab kuch loota jaayega."

---

## Tera AI Kaise Chalega? (The Pipeline)

Ab tu soch raha hoga ki ye saari rules AI kaise apply karega? Tera LLM (jaise GPT-4 ya Llama-3) directly code padh ke thodi na exact reentrancy path nikaal lega. Uski memory aur reasoning limit hoti hai.

Isliye hum pipeline banayenge:
1. **Input:** Raw Solidity Code.
2. **Parser:** Slither ya `solc` se AST + CFG generate karo (JSON format mein).
3. **AI Processing:** Tera fine-tuned LLM (Module 6 mein aayega) is AST JSON ko input lega. Kyunki JSON structured hai, AI ko samajh aayega ki kaunsa node kahan hai.
4. **Rule Engine:** AI apne training (jo hum Module 5 aur 6 mein karenge) ke based pe bolega ki "AST node 45 pe external call hai, aur node 47 pe state change hai. Reentrancy pattern match!"

> 🎯 **Key Insight:** Tera AI text nahi padh raha, wo AST structure padh raha hai. Isse false positives (galat alerts) bahut kam hote hain. AI ko pata hota hai ki `require` statement exactly kahan hai, `msg.sender` check kahan hai. Ye precision ka raaz hai, boss.

### Custom Script Ka Example (Python + Slither)

Dekh, tere liye ek chhota sa script jaisa logic deta hoon, samajh kaise aata hai. Tujhe Slither ka Python API use karna aana chahiye.

```python
from slither import Slither

# Slither ko code khila
slith = Slither('ChaiTapri.sol')

# Har contract mein ghus
for contract in slith.contracts:
    # Har function dekh
    for function in contract.functions:
        # Check kar koi external call toh nahi
        external_calls = [n for n in function.nodes if 'call' in str(n.type)]
        
        for call_node in external_calls:
            # Call ke baad state variable change ho raha hai?
            for state_var in function.state_variables_written:
                if state_var.node_id > call_node.node_id:
                    print(f"BC Reentrancy mil gaya! Function: {function.name}")
```

Ye basic logic hai. Tera AI model basically aisi hi logic ko neural network ke through automatically seekhega. Tu Slither ke built-in detectors use kar sakta hai, lekin asli power tab aayegi jab tu LLM ko AST nodes feed karke usse naye patterns detect karwayega jo Slither abhi tak nahi janta. Kyunki hackers roz naye tareeke sochte hain, aur Slither ka update late aata hai. Tera AI real-time seekhega.

---

## Aakhiri Gol

Saale, ye chapter padh ke soch mat ki tu auditor ban gaya. Ye toh bas map hai. Tujhe pata chala ki code ko kaise todna hai, uska X-ray (AST) kaise nikaalna hai, aur execution ka rasta (CFG) kaise track karna hai. 

Agar tujhe AST aur CFG ka concept clear nahi hai, toh Module 4 mein jab hum AI ko embeddings feed karenge, toh tera dimaak ghol jaayega. Static analysis bina AST ke, wahi hai jaise tu biryani kha raha ho but usmein salt daalna bhool gaya ho. Kuch fayda nahi.

> ☕ **Real Talk:** Slither ka documentation padh. Python mein Slither object print karke dekh ki usme kya-kya aata hai. Ek vulnerable contract le, uska AST JSON dekh. Dimaag mein ghus jayega. 

Aaj ke liye itna kaafi hai. Next chapter mein hum is AI ke dimaag ke peeche jayenge — LLMs aur Code Embeddings ka funda. Tab tak apne aapko ek vulnerable contract ka AST nikaal ke dekh.warna agli baar tapri pe aakar thappad padega. Chal ab nikal, kaam pe lag.

modules.title: AI ka Dimag - LLMs aur Code Embeddings Ka Fundoo Scene
modules.content:
Abe sun, raat ke 3 baj rahe hain. Teri aankhon mein neend hai, screen pe Solidity ka code hai, aur tu soch raha hai—"Bhai, AI toh sab kuch kar raha hai, chatgpt se poem likhwa lete hain, code likhwa lete hain, toh ye Smart Contract Auditor ka kaam kyun nahi kar paa raha?" 

Tu sochta hai ki AI ko bas code de denge aur wo bol dega, "Boss, yahan line 42 pe Reentrancy ka bug hai, sudhar le." BC, aisa hota toh ab tak sab crorepati ban gaye hote. Asli problem ye hai ki AI ko Solidity ka code samajh nahi aata. Wo usko bas angrezi ka text samajh leta hai. Tu biryani ke recipe mein dosa ka batter daal raha hai, toh kachra toh banega hi na!

Pichhle 3 chapters mein tune EVM ka internal khel (Storage, Memory, Calldata), DeFi ke hacks ka history (SWC-101, SWC-107), aur AST ka blueprint todhne ka talent seekha. Ab waqt hai AI ka dimag kholne ka. Aaj hum machine ko Solidity sikhaayenge. Transformers aur Embeddings ka asli funda samajh, warna saale tu AI auditor banane ki jagah AI joker bana raha hai. Collar pakad, chalu karte hain.

## Asli Funda (Core Concepts)

### 1. Code ko Text Mat Samajh, Uska "Meaning" Samajh (Embeddings)

Dekh bhai, computer angrezi mein baat nahi karta. Uska dimag sirf numbers samajhta hai. Jab tu ChatGPT ko bolta hai "Reentrancy", toh AI pehle is word ko ek number ke sequence mein badalta hai. Isko bolte hain **Embedding**. 

**Chai ki tapri wali explanation:** 
Embeddings aise samajh, jaise Mumbai ki local train ke dabbe. "Reentrancy" aur "Recursive Call" ek hi dabbe mein baithenge, kyunki unka matlab same hai. "Reentrancy" aur "ERC20 Token" alag-alag dabbe mein, kyunki inka connection direct nahi hai. AI har code snippet aur word ko ek 768-dimensional (ya uske aas-paas) space mein ek point ki tarah rakhta hai. Jiske coordinates closest honge, wo ek dusre se related honge.

Agar tu general NLP (Natural Language Processing) model use karega, toh wo "Mapping" ko Hindi ke map (naksha) samajh lega. Isliye hume **CodeBERT** ya **GraphCodeBERT** jaise transformers chahiye jo specifically code ke liye trained hain. Ye models code ke variables, functions aur unke relations ko map karte hain.

> 💡 **Pro Tip:** Generic text embeddings (jaise Word2Vec) Solidity pe fail honge. Hamesha code-specific pre-trained models (CodeBERT, UniXcoder) use kar, aur phir unko Solidity ke data par fine-tune kar (jo hum Module 6 mein karenge).

### 2. Tokenization aur Context Window - "Bhai Pet Bhar Gaya"

Saale, ek Smart Contract dekh, 500 lines ka hai. Tu AI ko bol, "Idhar se audit kar." AI ro dega. Kyun? Kyunki AI ka pet chhota hota hai. Isko bolte hain **Context Window Limit**.

Jaise tu 2 minute mein sirf 5 samose kha sakta hai, AI ek baar mein sirf utne hi "Tokens" process kar sakta hai. Token matlab word ka chhota tukda. "Reentrancy" ek token hai, lekin "uint256" do token ho sakta hai ("uint" aur "256").

Agar contract 10,000 tokens ka hai, aur tere model ki limit 4,096 tokens hai, toh AI contract ka aadha hissa bhool jayega. Jaise tu Mumbai se Delhi ki train mein sirf aadha ticket kharidta hai aur guard tujhe beech mein utaar deta hai. Wahi AI ke saath hota hai.

**Kaise handle karein is scene ko?**
- **Sliding Window:** Contract ko chhote-chhote pieces mein tod (function by function). Lekin isse context kho jata hai. Function A kya hai, Function B ko nahi pata.
- **Long-context Models:** Llama 3 ya Mistral jaise models aate hain 8k, 16k, ya 32k context window ke saath. Inko bada code pel sakte hain.
- **AST + Chunking:** (Yaad rakh Module 3 ka AST?). Code ko randomly mat tod. AST tree ke hisaab se tod. Ek function, uske modifiers, aur uske dependent variables ko ek chunk mein rakh. Tab AI ko context milega.

> ⚠️ **Common Mistake:** Randomly code ko 500-500 lines ke chunk mein baat dena. Bhai, ek function ka aadha hissa chunk A mein, aadha chunk B mein. AI dono ko jod nahi paayega aur bol dega "Syntax Error". Syntax error nahi hai, tera tod-ne ka tareeka galat hai.

### 3. Open-Source LLMs ka Asli Khel (Llama 3 aur Mistral)

GPT-4 api pe depend mat reh. Har baar cloud pe data bhejne mein time lagta hai, privacy ka panga hai, aur paise bhi jyada lagte hain. Asli hacker apna khud ka model local chalata hai. Isliye open-source LLMs ka entry hota hai.

**Llama 3 (Meta ka beta):** Ye aaj kal ki gully ka sabse bada don hai. 8 Billion parameters se lekar 70 Billion parameters tak. 8B chhota hai, local laptop pe chal jayega, lekin Solidity ke complex bugs pakadne mein thoda chhota pad jayega. 70B bada hai, dimag tez hai, lekin isko chalane ke liye GPU chahiye.

**Mistral (France ka don):** Ye model mixture-of-experts (MoE) use karta hai. Matlab ek bada dimag, par kaam ke time sirf zaroori hissa active rehta hai. Gas kam khata, jaldi uthta hai.

**Solidity par test kaise karein?**
Jab in models ko Solidity par test karta hai, toh inke 3 panga aate hain:
1. **Hallucination:** Model aise code ka bug dhoondh leta hai jo hai hi nahi. "Bhai yahan integer overflow ho sakta hai (SWC-101)." Tu check karega, code mein already `SafeMath` ya Solidity 0.8+ use kiya gaya hai. Model ne fekuwa maara.
2. **Incomplete Logic:** Model syntax samajh leta hai, par state changes ka logic miss kar deta hai. Piche chapter mein seekha—Checks-Effects-Interactions. Model kabhi-kabhi Effects pehle likhne ka suggestion de deta hai, par usko ye nahi pata ki ye specific function external call bhi kar raha hai.
3. **Tokenization Fails:** Solidity ke naye versions (0.8.x) ke keywords kabhi-kabhi in models ke vocabulary mein nahi hote. `unchecked` block ko wo galat samajh leta hain.

Isliye open-source model ko seedha audit ke liye mat bitha. Pehle isko Solidity sikha. Embeddings generate kar, AST se graph bana, aur model ko context do.

### 4. Code Embeddings aur Vector Databases - "AI ka Cheating Paper"

Bhai, agar AI ko har baar contract padh ke samajhna padega, toh wo exam mein fail ho jayega. Asli jugaad kya hai? **Vector Database**.

Jaise exam mein bachpan ke bachche chit (cheat sheet) le jaate the, waise AI ke liye vector DB chit ka kaam karta hai. 
Tere paas pehle se 1000 hacked contracts ke AST aur embeddings hain. Jab naya contract aata hai, AI uska embedding nikaalta hai, aur Vector DB (jaise Pinecone, ChromaDB, ya FAISS) mein check karta hai ki ye naya code kis purane code ke sabse close hai.

Agar naya code embedding mein `DAO hack` wale code ke 95% match kar raha hai, toh AI turant bol dega: "Abe ye toh Reentrancy (SWC-107) ka clone lag raha hai, yahan external call ke baad state change ho raha hai, State Changes after External Calls wala rule tod raha hai!"

> 🎯 **Key Insight:** AI ko sirf code mat de. Uske saath us code ka "Context" aur "Embedding" de. Vector DB ke through AI ko past hacks ki yaad dilata reh. Ye RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation) ka core hai, jiska asli setup hum Module 8 mein karenge.

## Practical Scene (Street-level Application)

Chal, theory band. Ab practically dekhte hain ki ye embeddings aur LLMs Solidity code par kaise lagte hain. 

Maan le tu ek Llama 3 8B model ko local chala raha hai. Tere paas ek vulnerable function hai. Pehle dekhte hain code ka tukda:

```solidity
// SPDX-License-Identifier: MIT
pragma solidity ^0.8.0;

contract VulnerableWallet {
    mapping(address => uint256) public balances;

    function deposit() external payable {
        balances[msg.sender] += msg.value;
    }

    function withdraw() external {
        uint256 bal = balances[msg.sender];
        require(bal > 0, "Khaali pet mat khich");
        
        // BUG: External call before state change (SWC-107)
        (bool success, ) = msg.sender.call{value: bal}("");
        require(success, "Transfer fail");
        
        balances[msg.sender] = 0;
    }
}
```

Agar tu general model ko ye code dega, toh wo bolega: "This is a wallet contract. It deposits and withdraws money. Looks fine." 
BC, fine kya laga raha hai? Reentrancy ki dhammi nikal rahi hai idhar!

Ab dekh Llama 3 ko kaise tackle karte hain. Hum isko prompt nahi denge. Hum isko Embeddings ka context denge. 

**Step 1: Code ko Tokenize aur Embed kar**
Hum CodeBERT model use karke is function ka vector nikaalenge. 
Function `withdraw()` ke andar ka logic:
- `balances[msg.sender]` (State read)
- `msg.sender.call{value: bal}` (External call)
- `balances[msg.sender] = 0` (State write)

**Step 2: Vector DB mein Check kar**
Vector DB mein is pattern ka embedding match karega. Aur paadegi, ye exactly SWC-107 (Reentrancy) ka pattern hai. Kyunki DB mein DAO attack ka code same sequence mein stored hai.

**Step 3: LLM ko Context ke saath feed kar**
Ab hum Llama 3 ko prompt denge, lekin us prompt mein pura contract nahi, sirf relevant AST chunk aur Vector DB se mila hua similar hack ka example denge.

*Prompt Structure for Llama 3:*
```
You are a Solidity Security Auditor.
Analyze the following function AST and logic.
Known Vulnerability Context (from Vector DB): 
[Insert DAO Hack Reentrancy Example here]

Target Function:
[Insert VulnerableWallet withdraw AST chunk here]

Question: Does the target function have any state change issues or external call vulnerabilities? Explain in 2 lines.
```

Ab Llama 3 ka dimag chalega. Wo dekhega ki context mein diya gaya example external call ke baad state change kar raha tha. Target function mein bhi same hai. Wo output dega:

*"Bhai (I mean, Boss), yahan `msg.sender.call` ke baad `balances[msg.sender] = 0` ho raha hai. Agar `msg.sender` ek malicious contract hai, toh wo `call` ke andar se tumhare `withdraw()` function ko dubara call kar dega aur balance kabhi 0 nahi hoga. Checks-Effects-Interactions rule tod diya. SWC-107 Reentrancy hai. Sudhar le nahi toh poora paisa ud jayega."*

Dekh difference? Randomly code pelne se AI bakwaas karta hai. Context aur Embeddings ke saath pelne se AI auditor ban jaata hai.

### Open-Source Model Testing Setup (Apne Haathon)

Tu soch raha hoga, "Bhai, ye sab test kaise karu?" 
Sun, ollama ya LM Studio download kar apne local machine pe. Llama 3 (8B) ko load kar. Phir Python ka script likh.

```python
import requests
import json

# Llama 3 local API endpoint
url = "http://localhost:11434/api/generate"

# Tera prompt with AST context
prompt_data = {
    "model": "llama3",
    "prompt": "Analyze this Solidity function for SWC-107: [withdraw function AST]",
    "stream": False
}

response = requests.post(url, json=prompt_data)
print(response.json()['response'])
```

Pehli baar jab tu isko chalayega, Llama 3 galat dega. Kyunki usko Solidity ka context nahi pata. Tu dekhega wo bol raha hai "Use require statement" (jo pehle se hai). Ya bol raha hai "Add SafeMath" (jo 0.8+ mein default hai). Tab tera kaam hai isko fine-tune karna. Tabhi hum Module 6 mein LoRA (Low-Rank Adaptation) ka danda chalayenge. Us din tu is generic Llama 3 ko Solidity ka don banayega.

> ☕ **Real Talk:** Local LLMs chalana koi mazaak nahi hai. 8B model hi agar Solidity samajh jaye, toh tere paas gold hai. Par shuru mein frustration ayegi. Model tujhe gaale dega apne jaise jawab deke. Patience rakh. Tokenization aur context window ka dhyan rakh, warna context overflow hoga aur model hallucinate karega.

### Tokenization ka Asli Scene Samajh

Ek aur baat. Llama 3 ka tokenizer Solidity ke kuch keywords ko galat tod deta hai. 
Jaise: `uint256` ko Llama 3 shayad `u`, `int`, `2`, `56` mein tod de. Iska matlab AI samajhega ki ye 4 alag cheezein hain. Isse embedding kachra ho jayega.

Iska solution kya hai? 
Tujhe tokenizer ko bhi Solidity ke hisaab se train karna padega. Ya phir, code ko pre-process karke AI ko dena padega. Jaise `uint256` ko `uint_256` bana dena, taaki model isko ek token samjhe. Ye chhoti si cheez hai, par asli gully ka hacker ishi chhoti cheez pe focus karta hai. Kyunki tokenization galat hui, toh embedding galat hui, embedding galat hui toh Vector DB galat match hua, aur match galat hua toh AI ne bol diya "Contract Safe Hai!" aur tera 10 million dollar ud gaya.

> ⚠️ **Common Mistake:** Code ko directly LLM API pe mat bhej. Pehle usko clean kar. Extra spaces hata, comments hata (agar context chahiye toh rakh, warna hata), aur complex types ko simplify kar. Context window bachao, BC!

### AI ka Dimag - Aakhri Gyaan

Bhai, AI jaadu nahi hai. Ye mathematics hai. Transformers attention mechanism use karte hain. Matlab jab AI `msg.sender.call` dekhta hai, toh uska attention `balances` variable par jaata hai. Agar model trained hai, toh wo link banayega. Nahi trained hai, toh wo `msg.sender.call` ko ek normal function call samajh ke ignore kar dega.

Tera kaam hai is attention ko sahi direction mein modhna. Open-source models (Llama 3, Mistral) tere control mein hain. Tu inke hyperparameters (temperature, top_p) khel sakta hai. 
- **Temperature 0.0:** Ratta maarta hai. Sirf wahi bolega jo usne seekha hai. Audit ke liye best hai.
- **Temperature 0.7:** Creative hai. Naye bugs dhoondh sakta hai, par bakwaas bhi kar sakta hai.

Audit ke time pe humesha low temperature rakh. Tujhe poet nahi, auditor chahiye.

### Summary of the Gully

1. **Embeddings:** Code ko numbers bana, Vector DB mein daal. Past hacks se match kara.
2. **Context Window:** Pet mat bharwa zyada. AST use kar, chhote chunks bana, context bacha.
3. **Open-Source Models:** Llama 3 aur Mistral ko local chala. Privacy aur speed milegi, par inko Solidity sikhaani padegi.
4. **Tokenization:** Code ko saaf kar ke model ko de. Random tokens mat todwa.

Agar tu ye 4 cheezein samajh gaya, toh tera AI auditor ka foundation solid hai. Module 5 mein hum is foundation ke upar data ka building banayenge. Kyun bhai? Bina data ke AI wahi karega jo tera pet kutta karta hai—bakwaas. 

Ab jaakar code chala, dekh kaise Llama 3 tere Solidity function ko roast karta hai. Agar error aaye, toh dimaag lagaa, API check kar, aur wapas aaja. 

> 🎯 **Key Insight:** AI auditing ka 80% kaam model ke dimag mein nahi, usko sahi context dena hai. Context diya embedding se, aur embedding aayi AST se. Ye chain tod mat dena.

Agli gully (Module 5) mein dataset ka khazana khodenge. Tab tak apna local model set kar, embeddings ka khel khel, aur tayar reh. Bole toh, dimag lagaa, nahi toh kachra ho jayega!

modules.title: Dataset ka Khazana - AI ko Asli Data Pilao
modules.content:
Abe sun na, ek kahani sunata hoon. Kal raat ko 3 baje ek bhaiya ro raha tha Twitter pe — "Mera AI auditor 10 lakh dollar ka smart contract audit kar raha tha, usne bola 'Contract is 100% safe, no issues found'. 2 ghante baad contract hack ho gaya, 500K UDSC drain ho gaya." Saale ne ChatGPT se direct prompt daala tha: "Find vulnerabilities in this Solidity code." BC, tera dimaag chaatne ke liye hai kya AI ka? 

Tu ne Module 4 mein LLMs aur embeddings ka funda toh samajh liya. Tera AI ab Solidity ka syntax pehchanta hai. Lekin ye machine tere jaisi hi hai — jo khata hai waisa bolta hai. Agar isko tu *The Bachelor* dekhega, toh ye wahi hoga. Agar isko tu *Dark Knight* dekhega, toh wahi hoga. Agar tere AI ko tu aadha-adhura, galat label wala data pilayega, toh ye aisa hi galat audit dega. 

Saale, ye itna important hai ki isko skip kiya toh tera career ka barbaad ho jaayega, pakka. Bina solid data ke tera AI ek jhola chhap hafte ke tantrik jaisa hai — jo bimari ka naam sun ke churan de dega, par asli test report nahi. Aaj hum sikhenge is AI ko asli khurchan (scraping) kaise khilani hai.

## Asli Funda (Core Concepts)

Dekh bhai, ye aise samajh, jaise tu Mumbai ki local train mein Virar fast mein chadhta hai. Agar tujhe pata hai ki local train kaise chalti hai (LLM architecture), toh bahut badiya. Par agar tujhe pata nahi ki kahan se kahan tak ka track hai, kahan pe raasta katna hai, aur kahan pe signal red hai (Dataset), toh direct Borivali mein utar ke platform pe hi gaadi ke neeche aa jayega. Data wahi track hai. 

**1. Scrape Karna: Etherscan aur DefiLlama pe Jugaad**
Tujhe data kahan se milega? Etherscan se. Lekin Etherscan ka API itna slow hai ki beech raastey mein so jayega. Tu API ka wait karega ya direct HTML scrape karega? Asli gully ka don API ki limit pe rely nahi karta. Wo Etherscan ke verified contracts ke pages ko scrape karta hai. Har contract ka source code utha, uska address note kar, aur compiler version (like `0.8.0`) ka label laga. 

DefiLlama se kya? DefiLlama tujhe batayega ki kaunsa protocol TVL (Total Value Locked) mein sabse upar hai. Wo protocols jinka TVL 100 million dollar plus hai, unke contracts tujhe asli data denge. Aur jo hacks huye hain (Rekt database se), wo tere "Buggy" data ke king hain. 

> 💡 **Pro Tip:** Etherscan pe `solc` version aur contract size (lines of code) ke saath data ko bucket kar. Agar tera AI sirf 500 lines ke contract pe train hua, aur tu usko 5000 lines ka Uniswap V3 de dega, toh wo halwa ho jayega. Balance rakh, boss.

**2. Labeling: Buggy vs Not Buggy (Asli Kartoos)**
Ab tere paas 10,000 contracts hain. Ab inko label karna hai. Ye safe hai ya buggy? 
Saale, yahan 99% log fail ho jate hain. Wo GitHub pe "Solidity vulnerable contracts" wala repo download kar lete hain aur usko "Buggy" mark kar dete hain. BC, wo repo 2018 ka hai. `SafeMath` aur `Checked Arithmetic` ka zamana aagaya hai, aur tu usi purane kachre se AI ko sikha raha hai? 

Asli labeling aise hoti hai:
- **Buggy Contracts:** Hacked protocols (E.g., Curve, Wormhole, Nomad). Inke pre-hack commit hash uthao. SWC Registry se known vulnerable patterns nikaal. Har bug ko `SWC-101: Integer Overflow and Underflow` ya `SWC-107: Reentrancy` jaise tags se mark kar. 
- **Not Buggy Contracts:** Top 100 DeFi protocols jo 2+ saal se chal rahe hain aur jinka audit OpenZeppelin ya Trail of Bits ne kiya hai. Inko "Safe" mark kar. Lekin dhyaan rakh — "audited" matlab 100% safe nahi hota, par baseline ke liye ye best hai.

> ⚠️ **Common Mistake:** Pura contract ek hi label dena. Ek contract mein ek function vulnerable ho sakta hai aur baaki 99 safe. Agar tune pure contract ko "Buggy" label diya, tera AI confuse ho jayega ki "Bhai, kya in 99 lines mein bug hai, ya uss 1 line mein?" Function-level labeling kar, chote-chote tukde karna machine ko pasand hai.

**3. Data Cleaning aur Augmentation: Hallucination Roko**
Ab tera data ready hai. Lekin machine abhi bhi bakwas karega. Kyun? Kyunki tere data mein kachra hai. 
- **Cleaning:** Comments hata de code se. Kuch chutiy developers code mein likhte hain `// TODO: fix this reentrancy later`. Tera AI comment padh ke direct "Reentrancy found!" bol dega. Machine ko sirf logic chahiye, commentary nahi. 
- **Augmentation:** Tere paas 1000 buggy contracts hain, 90,000 safe hain. Ye imbalance hai. Agar tera AI har baar "Safe" bolega toh uski accuracy 99% dikhegi, par wo asli hack miss kar dega. Isliye augmentation karna padta hai. Existing bugs ko mutate kar — variable names change kar, `uint256` ko `uint128` kar, loop ka logic thoda modify kar. Jugaad laga, synthetic bugs bana. 

> ☕ **Real Talk:** Data cleaning aur labeling mein 80% time lagta hai. Ise boring mat samajh. Tu abhi jo mehnat kar raha hai, na, wo tera AI kal teri maa ki tarah bachayega public humiliation se. 

## Practical Scene (Street-level Application)

Chal, ab collar pakadke asli kaam pe aate hain. Theory band, practical shuru. 

### Step 1: Scrape aur Structure
Maan le tujhe Reentrancy wale contracts chahiye. Tujhe pata hai ki DAO hack (2016) history ka sabse bada reentrancy tha. Par ek data ka kaam nahi chalega. Tujhe diverse data chahiye.

```python
# Bhai, ye pseudo-code hai dimaag lagane ke liye
import requests
from bs4 import BeautifulSoup

def scrape_etherscan(contract_address):
    url = f"https://etherscan.io/address/{contract_address}#code"
    # Etherscan thoda strict hai, headers toh daal
    headers = {'User-Agent': 'Mozilla/5.0 (Gully Boy)'}
    response = requests.get(url, headers=headers)
    soup = BeautifulSoup(response.text, 'html.parser')
    
    # Source code nikalo
    source_code = soup.find('pre', {'class': 'js-sourcecopyarea'}).text
    
    # Compiler version nikalo (bohot zaroori hai)
    version = "0.8.0" # parse it properly
    
    return {
        "address": contract_address,
        "code": source_code,
        "compiler": version,
        "label": "Unknown" # abhi unknown hai, baad mein tag karenge
    }
```

### Step 2: Labeling ka Asli Khel
Ab tere paas raw code hai. Isko label kaise kare? Manual nahi kar sakta 10,000 contracts. Yahan heuristic use kar. Slither jaise static analysis tools chala apne scraped data pe. Agar Slither bolta hai ki `SWC-107: Reentrancy` hai, toh usko `Potential_Buggy` tag de. Phir manually top 100 check kar le.

> 🎯 **Key Insight:** Slither perfect nahi hai. Slither jo "Buggy" bolega, usme 30% false positives honge. Lekin tera goal yahan data gather karna hai, perfect banne ka nahi. Slither ke false positives ko filter karke tere AI ko sikhana hai ki "bhai, ye wala pattern actual mein safe hai, alert mat dena." Isko tujhe Module 9 mein detail mein samajhna hai.

### Step 3: Augmentation - Tera AI kya seekhega?
Agar tujhe Reentrancy sikhani hai, toh tujhe AI ko dikhana padega ki kaise dushman (attacker) code ko bypass karta hai. Ek safe contract lo, aur usme jaan-बूझकर bug daal.

**Original Safe Code (Checks-Effects-Interactions follow karta hai):**
```solidity
function withdraw(uint _amount) public {
    require(balances[msg.sender] >= _amount, "Kam balance hai saale");
    balances[msg.sender] -= _amount; // Effect
    (bool success, ) = msg.sender.call{value: _amount}(""); // Interaction
    require(success, "Transfer fail");
}
```

**Augmented Buggy Code (Tere dataset ke liye synthetic bug):**
```solidity
function withdraw(uint _amount) public {
    require(balances[msg.sender] >= _amount, "Kam balance hai saale");
    (bool success, ) = msg.sender.call{value: _amount}(""); // Interaction pehle!
    balances[msg.sender] -= _amount; // Effect baad mein! SWC-107 trigger!
    require(success, "Transfer fail");
}
```
Ab is bug ko tu 100 alag-alag tarike se likh sakta hai. Variable names badal (`balances` ki jagah `userFunds`), require message change kar. Tera AI ko 100 variations mil jayenge. Wo seekh jayega ki "Bhai, agar `call` se pehle state update nahi hua, toh pakka Reentrancy hai."

### Step 4: CWE aur SWC Tags ka Pyaar
Data labeling sirf 0 aur 1 (Buggy/Not Buggy) ka khel nahi hai. Tu AI ko multi-class classification sikha raha hai. Har bug ko uske asli naam se pukar. 

Jab tu data label kare, toh JSON format use kar. Machine ko JSON pasand hai, XML nahi.

```json
{
  "contract_name": "LendAndBorrow",
  "function": "flashLoan",
  "vulnerability": "SWC-107: Reentrancy",
  "severity": "High",
  "description": "State changes after external calls. Flash loan callback allows re-entry before balance update."
}
```

Dekh, tere AI ka dimaag khaali slate hai. Tere annotations hi uski duniya banayenge. Agar tune description mein bakwaas likhi, toh tera AI bhi wahi bakwas dega.

### Step 5: Hallucination ka Kaala Jaadu
LLMs ko hallucinate karna apne khoon mein hai. Agar tere training data mein ek bug galat label ho gaya, toh tera AI usko "Safe" bolega aur real world mein logon ke paise doob jayenge. 

Hallucination rokne ka ek hi asli tareeka hai — **High Quality Data**. 
- Duplicate contracts hata. Etherscan pe bahut contracts hain jo ek hi master contract ka clone hain (jaise Uniswap V2 ke 10,000 forks). Agar tune sabko rakh liya, toh tera AI Uniswap ke pattern ko overfit kar jayega. Kisi aur dekh lega naya bug.
- Compiler version ke hisaab se data alag kar. Solidity 0.8.0 mein integer overflow automatically revert hota hai (`SWC-101: Integer Overflow and Underflow` ka issue nahi raha). Agar tera AI 0.8.0 wale contract mein overflow ka bug dhoond raha hai, toh wo galat hai. Tujhe AI ko compiler version context dena padega.

> 💡 **Pro Tip:** Data prepare karte waqt ek validation set alag rakh. 10% data ko apne paas chhupa ke rakh. Jab tera AI training khatam kar le, toh uspe ye 10% data test kar. Agar ispe accuracy 90% se kam aayi, toh samajh le tera data hi kachra tha.

### Step 6: The "Not Buggy" Trap
Sab log buggy contracts dhoondhte hain, par "Not Buggy" contracts kahan se laun? 
Bhai, OpenZeppelin ki library dekh. Wo log world ke best auditors hain. Unka code 100% safe maan. Usko scrape kar aur label "Safe". Par ek dhyaan rakhe — OpenZeppelin ke contracts utility contracts hote hain (jaise `Ownable.sol`, `ERC20.sol`). Asli DeFi protocols complex hote hain. Tujhe safe complex contracts bhi chahiye. Yearn Finance, Compound, Aave ke audited contracts utha. Inko "Safe" mark kar.

### Step 7: Context is King (Metadata)
Abe, code sirf text nahi hai. Code ek environment hai. Agar tu AI ko sirf Solidity file de raha hai, toh wo adhura hai. Machine ko bataya jaaye ki ye contract karta kya hai.

```json
{
  "code": "...",
  "metadata": {
    "protocol_type": "Lending",
    "external_calls": true,
    "has_eth_receive": true,
    "uses_oracle": "Chainlink"
  }
}
```
Agar `protocol_type` "Lending" hai aur `uses_oracle` true hai, toh AI apne aagdhe dimaag se seekhega ki yahan `Oracle Dependencies` wale hacks ka chance zyada hai. Usko pehle se context mil jayega. Ye asli pro-level dataset banane ka raaz hai.

---

Bhai, tera data abhi bhi raw hai. Tujhe isko tokenize karna hoga, embeddings banana hoga (Module 4 yaad karna). Lekin embedding se pehle, tera data clean hona chahiye. 

**Aakhri Gyaan:**
Tu soch raha hoga ki "Bhai, itna kaam kaun kare? Direct internet se koi ready-made dataset download kar leta hoon." 
Saale, agar tujhe generic chahiye, toh ready-made le le. Par tujhe asli aukaat dikhani hai. Tere AI ko asli gully ka data chahiye, jisme latest hacks hon, latest compiler versions hon. Agar tu ye mehnat nahi karega, toh kal koi 17 saal ka hacker tere AI ko chootiya bana ke 10 million dollar loot lega, aur tu Twitter pe apology thread likhta phirega.

> ☕ **Real Talk:** Dataset building is a thankless job. Isme koi glory nahi hai. Jab log Hugging Face pe model dekhte hain, unhe sirf model ka size aur accuracy dikhti hai. Pichhe kisi bande ne 3 mahine Etherscan ki HTML parse kar ke bita diye, ye kisi ko nahi pata. Par asli hero wo hai. Aaj tu wo hero ban. Data jodo, saaf kar, label kar. 

Agar tujhe ye chapter padh ke lag raha hai ki "Abe itna toh main kar sakta hoon", toh karke dikha. Agar skip kiya toh... tera AI kal tweet marega: "I am sorry, I cannot let you do that." 

Uth, Etherscan khol, scraping ka script likh. Asli data ka khazana tere haath mein hai. Samjha? Nahi samjha? Padh dubara, dimag lagaa. Module 6 mein milte hain, jab is kachre ko LoRA ke through AI ke dimaag mein ghusayenge. Tab tak apna data sambhaal!

modules.title: LoRA aur Fine-Tuning - Asli Ghode Ko Sawar Karna
modules.content:
Abe sun, raat ke 3 baj rahe hain. Tu Mumbai local ki 6:47 ki fast train mein Virar se Churchgate ja raha hai. Peak hour. Bheed itni ki tera ek pair zameen pe nahi hai, dusra pair kisi uncle ke chappal pe hai. Bheed mein ek aawaz aati hai, "Bhaiya neeche utro, agla station Borivali!" Tu sochta hai, "BC main toh Andheri utarna tha, ye Borivali kahan se beech mein aa gaya?"

Bilkul ye hi scene hai tere generic LLM (ChatGPT ya Llama) ka. Tu usko Solidity ka code deta hai aur bolta hai "Bhai, isme vulnerability dhund." Wo model Andheri ka hai, par tujhe Borivali pe utar ke bolta hai "Haan bhai, ye code safe hai!" Saale ne Solidity ka logic toh samajh liya, par DeFi ke actual attacks (Reentrancy, Flash loan manipulation) ki gully ka raasta nahi pata. Wo general knowledge pe bakwas karega, tujhe exact vulnerability nahi milega.

Pichhle 5 chapters mein tune AST todha, embeddings banaye, dataset ka bhandara khola. Par ab tak tu woh mawali hai jo gyaan toh deta hai, par asli street fight nahi ladta. Generic AI se kaam nahi chalega bhai. Asli auditor banne ke liye, tere model ko aukaat dikhani padegi. Asli ghode ko sawar karna padega. Iska funda hai—**Fine-Tuning aur LoRA (Low-Rank Adaptation)**. 

Saale, ye chapter itna important hai ki isko skip kiya toh tera AI career ka barbaad ho jaayega, pakka. Full power ke saath chal.

---

## Asli Funda (Core Concepts)

Dekh bhai, fine-tuning aise samajh, jaise tu Mumbai ki street pe ek "Vada Pav" ka thela lagata hai. Tu general LLM ko ek "Master Chef" samajh jo French cuisine banata hai. Ab tu uss Master Chef ko apni Mumbai ki gully mein laaya. Usko French fries ke bajaye Vada Pav banana sikhaana hai. 

Agar tu usko scratch se sikhayega (Full Fine-Tuning), toh saala itna paisa aur GPU kharch hoga ki tera startup pehle hi band ho jaayega. Isliye hum use karenge **PEFT (Parameter-Efficient Fine-Tuning)** aur uska sabse khatarnaak banda—**LoRA**.

### LoRA ka Desi Jugaad

LoRA ka full form hai Low-Rank Adaptation. Aise samajh: Tere paas ek 1000 page ki bhari book hai (Pre-trained LLM ke billions of parameters). Ab tujhe usme Solidity ke bugs ke baare mein sirf 10 page ka addendum jodna hai. Tu poori book ko kyun phaadega? LoRA sirf wahi 10 page chipka deta hai, baaki book ko as-is rehne deta hai.

Mathematically, LoRA karta kya hai? Original model ke weights matrix $W$ ko freeze (ice) kar deta hai. Aur uske side mein do chhote matrices $A$ aur $B$ jod deta hai. $A$ dimensions reduce karta hai, $B$ wapas original dimension pe laata hai. 

$\Delta W = A \times B$

Training ke time sirf $A$ aur $B$ update hote hain. Original $W$ nahi chhedta. Result? Tere GPU ka aadha rent bach gaya, model fast hua, aur tera Vada Pav ban gaya!

> 💡 **Pro Tip:** Hamesha yaad rakh, LoRA original model ko overwrite nahi karta. Wo ek side-kick (adapter) hai. Jab tu inference karega, tab base model + LoRA adapter dono ko memory mein load karna padega. Par training ke time 99% VRAM bach jaata hai. Jugaad level: Mumbai.

### Instruction Dataset: Model ko "Audit this code" Sikhana

Bhai, model ko bina data ke sikhaane ki koshish karna, woh baat kisi tarah hai jaise autorickshaw wale ko "Nepal chalo" bolna. Khaali petrol pe nahi chalega, mileage nahi milega. Tujhe ek solid **Instruction Dataset** banana hoga.
